explicit_boundary.statement: This artifact records Product Owner review states for rec-002. accepted_for_decision entries exist, but accepted_for_decision only means the candidate may proceed to a future decision log task; needs_testing only means testing is required before any decision-log task. No deck change is authorized. No decision log entry is created. No new deck version is created. rec-002.json and rec-002.md remain unmodified by this artifact.

review_entries.rationale: Product Owner accepts this candidate for the decision-log path because the baseline identifies colored-cast consistency as a structural pressure point, and City of Brass / Mana Confluence directly address colored access without changing the deck yet.
review_entries.testing_notes: No separate testing gate is required before decision-log drafting, but final add/swap details still require a decision log and new deck version.

review_entries.rationale: Product Owner accepts this candidate for the decision-log path because Urza's Saga is aligned with the artifact-engine identity and supports artifact utility without itself authorizing a deck change.
review_entries.testing_notes: No separate testing gate is required before decision-log drafting, but final add/swap details still require a decision log and new deck version.

review_entries.rationale: Product Owner wants testing before advancing Krark-Clan Ironworks because it may significantly increase combo-engine explosiveness and shift the deck identity.
review_entries.testing_notes: Test whether KCI improves explosive turns without making the deck feel too deterministic or drifting away from the intended Myr artifact identity.

review_entries.rationale: Product Owner wants testing before advancing Mana Echoes because it is highly synergistic with Myr density but may be board-dependent or win-more.
review_entries.testing_notes: Test whether Mana Echoes consistently creates meaningful Myr-driven mana bursts or only performs when the board is already developed.

review_entries.rationale: Product Owner accepts this candidate for the decision-log path because Tezzeret the Seeker is aligned with the artifact-engine plan and can act as tutor, untap engine, or artifact-scaling threat without changing the deck yet.
review_entries.testing_notes: No separate testing gate is required before decision-log drafting, but final add/swap details still require a decision log and new deck version.
